narration_text: Now we'll see how the process of sending probes works, let's think about this together. Notice that the sender is transmitting three packets, each with a specific time-to-live value, which will reach a particular router on the path to the destination. Look at the diagram on your slide, observe how the packets are being sent and returned, and notice the time interval being measured. This process allows the sender to gauge the distance to each router. I want you to focus on the visual representation of this process, see how the packets are moving, and understand how this helps in determining the path. As we analyze this, keep in mind that this concept is crucial for understanding network topology, and we'll build on this idea in the next slide.
